2603.19742.title: Dual Path Attribution: Efficient Attribution for SwiGLU-Transformers through Layer-Wise Target Propagation
2603.19742.paper_summary: Understanding the internal mechanisms of transformer-based large language models (LLMs) is crucial for their reliable deployment and effective operation. While recent efforts have yielded a plethora of attribution methods attempting to balance faithfulness and computational efficiency, dense component attribution remains prohibitively expensive. In this work, we introduce Dual Path Attribution (DPA), a novel framework that faithfully traces information flow on the frozen transformer in one forward and one backward pass without requiring counterfactual examples. DPA analytically decomposes and linearizes the computational structure of the SwiGLU Transformers into distinct pathways along which it propagates a targeted unembedding vector to receive the effective representation at each residual position. This target-centric propagation achieves O(1) time complexity with respect to the number of model components, scaling to long input sequences and dense component attribution. Extensive experiments on standard interpretability benchmarks demonstrate that DPA achieves state-of-the-art faithfulness and unprecedented efficiency compared to existing baselines.
2603.19742.paper_summary_zh: 理解基於Transformer的大型語言模型（LLMs）的內部機制對於其可靠部署和有效運作至關重要。雖然最近的努力產生了大量的歸因方法，試圖在忠實性和計算效率之間取得平衡，但密集組件歸因仍然過於昂貴。在這項工作中，我們介紹了雙路徑歸因（DPA），這是一個新穎的框架，能夠在不需要反事實例的情況下，忠實地追蹤凍結Transformer中的信息流，通過一次前向和一次後向傳播。DPA 從分析上分解並線性化 SwiGLU Transformers 的計算結構，將其分為不同的路徑，沿著這些路徑傳播一個目標的去嵌入向量，以在每個殘差位置接收有效表示。這種以目標為中心的傳播在模型組件數量上達到了 O(1) 的時間複雜度，能夠擴展到長輸入序列和密集組件歸因。在標準可解釋性基準上的廣泛實驗表明，DPA 在忠實性和前所未有的效率方面超越了現有的基準。
2603.19742.author: Lasse Marten Jantsch et.al.
2603.19742.authors: Lasse Marten Jantsch, Dong-Jae Koh, Seonghyeon Lee, Young-Kyoon Suh
